spec_match.rationale: Page claims to ask the SW for clients.matchAll().length but never registers a Service Worker. The button logs window.frames.length and synthesises the SW count as frames.length+1.

interactive.rationale: Two iframes mount and the ask button fires, but no SW involved. The srcdoc inline probe always reports controller=none.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Promises asking the parent how many clients the SW sees — but no SW exists.

edge_cases.rationale: No real SW means no real client tree to exercise.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables, semantic pills, responsive scene grid.

openQuestions.title: Register a real SW and call clients.matchAll()
openQuestions.detail: Replace synthetic count with a real SW plus postMessage round-trip. See srcdoc-client-tree sibling for the correct pattern.

openQuestions.title: Fix the srcdoc inline controller probe
openQuestions.detail: Always reports controller=none because no SW is registered. Fixing SW registration unblocks this.

openQuestions.title: Show the postMessage path
openQuestions.detail: Blurb mentions the SW couldn't postMessage into srcdoc — add a SW-to-srcdoc message demo.

openQuestions.title: Replace speculative log with measured data
openQuestions.detail: Current log says SW would report N clients — replace with actual numbers.

summary: Most important concept of the feature is currently weakest — it fabricates the SW client count instead of asking a real SW.
